description: Inside a coffee shop, Joey and Chandler sit on an orange couch. Chandler reads a newspaper while Joey watches him.
Joey extends his hand toward the newspaper and asks, "Can I see the comics?"
Chandler lowers the newspaper and turns his head to look at Joey.
Chandler says, "This is the New York Times."
Joey gestures toward Chandler with an open hand.
Joey turns his palms up and asks, "Okay? May I see the comics?" Chandler leans his head back against the couch and touches his hair.
Phoebe, Monica, and Rachel walk into the coffee shop. Phoebe wears a yellow coat, Monica wears a black coat, and Rachel wears a black top.
Monica says, "Guys, I thought you were taking Ross to the game." Chandler replies, "We are. He's meeting us here." Monica begins, "No, Rachel is meeting us"
Monica finishes, "here." Phoebe gestures and says, "Oh, come on. They can be in the same room."
Joey points a finger and says, "Yeah, you should have been there last night." Monica asks, "Why? What happened now?"
Joey gestures with his hands and begins, "Well, Ross"
Joey continues, "was hanging out over at our place."

Joey says, "Rachel comes over to borrow some moisturizer from Chandler." Chandler forcefully swats Joey's arm with the rolled-up newspaper, making a loud smack sound. Joey draws his shoulder back quickly.
Chandler stares fixedly and leans forward toward Joey.
Chandler hits the newspaper against his other hand and says, "How hard is it to say something? Rachel came over to borrow something?"
Phoebe and Monica stand nearby, keeping their gaze on Joey.
Joey gestures with both hands and says, "Anyway, her and Ross just started yelling at each other."
Phoebe asks, "Wait, why was he yelling at her? He's the one who slept with someone else."
Joey says, "Well, I guess he says that because they were on a break when it happened that she should have forgiven him by now."
Phoebe raises her hands and says, "Whoa, he is"
Phoebe continues, "so unreasonable."
Phoebe adds, "God. Although I think I understand what he means," her facial expression changing to a slight grimace.
Chandler looks upward. Phoebe clasps her hands together.
Phoebe gestures broadly and says, "Oh, my God. This is like 60 minutes. Okay, at first, you're really mad"
Phoebe continues, "at that pharmaceutical company for making the drug. And"
Phoebe says, "then you just feel bad for the people because they needed to make their hair grow," lowering her hands to her sides.
Chandler taps the newspaper against his hand.
Chandler says, "You know what this is like? This is like when my parents got divorced."
Chandler holds the newspaper and stares straight ahead without moving.
Chandler says, "Man, I hope Ross doesn't try to kidnap me after the cub scouts."
Phoebe steps closer to the couch.
Phoebe gestures and says, "You know, I had a dream that Ross and Rachel were still together. They never broke up, and we were all just, like,"
Phoebe continues, "hanging out and everyone was happy."
Joey says, "I had the same dream." Chandler says, "Yeah, and nobody slept with that Xerox girl." Joey shakes his head and adds, "Oh, I had the opposite dream."
Chandler turns his head to stare closely at Joey.
Chandler says, "You know what?"
Chandler continues, "Maybe it's going to be okay. I mean, it's been a week." Joey replies, "Yeah, it's never taken me more than a week to get over a relationship."
Monica turns to look at Joey.
Monica says, "It's never taken you more than a shower to get over a relationship." Joey nods with a closed-mouth smile.
Rachel walks back into the group wearing a black top and a patterned skirt.
Rachel claps her hands and says, "Okay, let's go. Let's hit the road. Let's get the show on it."
Monica says, "Let me just get a cup of coffee." Rachel points and says, "Monica, I know the best coffee house, and it's so close."
Monica asks, "Closer than here?"
Rachel replies, "Okay."
Phoebe picks up a dark mug from a nearby table and says, "Look, I found coffee."
Phoebe extends the mug toward Rachel. Rachel begins, "Okay, let's get"
Rachel touches the mug, then pulls her hand back and says, "hey, I'm not going to drink somebody's old coffee." Phoebe retracts the mug.
Phoebe says, "Okay, Your Highness," holding the mug with both hands.
Rachel turns as Ross enters the coffee shop wearing a green corduroy jacket. They stop and face each other.
The rest of the group stands silently watching them. Ross and Rachel stare at each other without moving.

Phoebe speaks in a deepened voice, "Rachel, I'm really sorry." Changing to a higher pitch, she says, "That's okay. Ross, you want to get back together?" In the deepened voice, "Yeah." In the higher pitch, "Okay."
Phoebe turns her head slightly and asks, "Did anyone else hear that?" The others stare at her.
The screen turns completely black, and the audio goes silent.
The setting changes to an apartment living room. Joey sits in a black recliner chair, eating Chinese food from a white carton using chopsticks.
Joey lifts noodles with his chopsticks, opens his mouth, and misses catching the food.
Joey successfully places a bite of food into his mouth. Chandler is now visible standing by the kitchen counter, with Monica and Phoebe sitting on stools nearby.
Rachel walks into the kitchen area and approaches Chandler. Chandler turns to face her.
Rachel asks, "Is he here?"
Chandler answers, "No." Rachel hands Chandler a small bottle and says, "Oh, here's your moisturizer."
Chandler takes the bottle and looks at it. Rachel walks past him toward the living area.
Rachel says, "Hi."
Rachel points at herself and says, "You guys are going to love me. Okay, check it out."
Rachel holds up five tickets and says, "Thursday night, five tickets. Calvin Klein lingerie show."
Rachel points her finger toward the group and says, "And you guys are coming with me."
Joey, Phoebe, and Chandler look at Rachel with widened eyes and slightly parted lips.

In an apartment kitchen, Rachel and Chandler stand near each other. Rachel holds her hands out, palms up. Joey stands nearby eating from a Chinese takeout box. Phoebe and Monica sit at the counter eating.
Rachel gestures with her hands and says, "I said that out loud, right?"
Chandler and Rachel maintain their positions.
Chandler points to himself and says, "Yes, it's just that we kind of already made plans with Ross."
Rachel touches her neck and says "Oh," then clasps her hands in front of her. Joey, Phoebe, and Monica watch her.
Rachel says, "Okay, well, there you go." Phoebe says, "No, it's just that he got this new home theater dealy and he wants us to check it out." Chandler gestures and says, "Yeah, he's..."
Chandler finishes, "...really excited about it, too. He even recorded Showtimes on his answer." Monica motions toward Chandler.
Monica gestures for Chandler to stop.
Monica says, "Honey."
Rachel turns toward the door and says, "Oh, okay."
Joey steps toward Rachel and says, "Rach. It's not that we don't want to. Really? Are we talking models in their underwear?"
Joey points his chopsticks at Rachel.
Joey asks, "And heels?"
Rachel looks at Joey with her mouth slightly open. Joey continues to eat from his takeout box. Chandler stands with his hands on his hips.
Joey points at Rachel with his spoon and says, "Ross did ask us first, and we set that night aside." Rachel says, "No." Joey adds, "Hey, come on. You know if he asked you first. That's only fair."
Rachel turns and walks out the apartment door. Chandler walks into the living area, saying "Ah" while rubbing his chest.
Phoebe rubs her chest and says, "Boy, I feel bad. Boy."
Phoebe says, "Yeah, very bad," then looks toward the living area. Chandler stands near a shelf, lighting a cigarette. Phoebe asks, "Chandler, what are you doing? Chandler."
Monica looks toward Chandler and says, "Oh, my God."
Chandler turns, holding the lit cigarette.
Monica asks, "You're smoking again?"
Chandler holds the cigarette and gestures with his other hand, saying, "Well, actually, yesterday I was smoking again. Today I'm smoking still."
Phoebe stands up from her stool and walks toward Chandler.
Phoebe asks, "Why would you start again after chewing all that quitting gum?" Chandler replies, "I'm telling you, this is just like my parents..."
Chandler continues, "...divorce. Which is when I started smoking in the first place."
Monica and Phoebe stand facing Chandler.
Monica asks, "Weren't you nine?"
Chandler places the cigarette in his mouth and takes a drag, looking away.
Chandler exhales a cloud of smoke and says, "Yeah."
Monica and Phoebe stare at Chandler.
Chandler points his cigarette toward them and says, "I'm telling you something, that first smoke after nap time..." He brings the cigarette back to his mouth.
The apartment buzzer sounds. Chandler jumps, runs around the armchair, and throws his cigarette toward the kitchen sink.
Chandler says, "oh, that's great."
Chandler walks toward the door and says, "With my luck, that's going to be him." Phoebe asks, "Him? Him?"
Phoebe looks toward Chandler.
Phoebe asks, "Ross?" Chandler points to the door and says, "No, him."
Chandler stands by the door.
Chandler says, "253. His eyes are on the sparrow."
Chandler remains standing near the door, looking toward Phoebe and Monica.
Chandler says, "My parents got divorced. That's when I started using humor as a defense mechanism," gesturing with his hand.
Chandler turns and walks back toward the kitchen. Rachel opens the door and enters the apartment.
Rachel stands in the doorway and says, "Hi. Do you guys have plans for the weekend? Because I have my sister on hold and she said that we could have her cabin..."
Rachel lowers her hands.
Rachel gestures enthusiastically and says, "...for the weekend and go skiing."
Rachel looks at the group expectantly.
Rachel gestures to herself and asks, "I'm asking you first, right? I mean, I'm playing by the rules."
Chandler says, "Absolutely."
Rachel claps her hands together and smiles.
Rachel sniffs the air, points at Chandler, and says, "Chandler, you're smoking. What are you doing?" Chandler replies, "Hey, shut up. You're not my real mom," and walks out of the room.
Outside, a city skyline is visible at night.
Inside the apartment, Joey and Phoebe sit at the kitchen counter. Ross stands in the living area, and Chandler sits at the dining table near an open window, smoking a cigarette.
Joey rubs his chest and says, "Hey, can you close that window? Chandler? My nipples could cut glass over here."
Chandler looks at Joey and takes a drag from his cigarette.
Phoebe points at Joey and says, "Wait, really?"
Phoebe adds, "Because mine get me out of tickets."
Ross walks toward the living area holding a large bowl. Monica sits on the floor nearby.
Ross stands and addresses the group, saying, "Look, you guys, I just want to say I really, really appreciate you spending this time with me. It's been a pretty hard time right now,"
Ross finishes, "...so I just want to say thanks." Chandler points at Ross with his cigarette and says, "Can somebody else hug him? I have to stay by the window."
Ross places the bowl down on the coffee table.
Ross claps his hands and says, "Oh, hey, how about this weekend we have a laser disc marathon? Okay? And maybe a tournament..."
Ross walks over to a wooden cabinet on the wall.
Ross opens the cabinet to reveal a dartboard and asks, "on my new dartboard. What do you think?"
Ross gestures toward the dartboard and says, "Had two days of darts."
Joey says, "It'll be great."
Chandler stands up from his chair. Joey points toward Ross.

Joey says, "It'll be great for next weekend." Ross gestures with his hands and replies, "No, this weekend. Guys." Chandler walks over to stand next to Joey and says, "It'll be great for next weekend."
Chandler nods and adds, "I mean, it'll be great."
Ross looks toward Chandler with a still expression.
Inside an apartment, Phoebe and Joey stand near the kitchen counter. Phoebe turns her head.
Ross walks into the living room, joining Monica and Chandler. Ross asks, "What's going on?" Phoebe, standing near Joey, answers, "We were sort of invited to go skiing."
Phoebe gestures with her right hand as she continues, "You know, Rachel's sister's cabin." Ross places his hands in his pockets.
Ross nods slightly, looking down and then back up. He takes one hand out of his pocket.
Ross gestures with his hands and asks, "So for the whole weekend?"
Monica steps forward and places her hand on Ross's arm.
Monica says, "We're really sorry, but she did ask us first," gesturing with both hands.
Ross looks at Monica, keeping his hands loosely clasped.
Ross says, "Yeah, that's okay. I mean, if you guys all have to go away for the first weekend I'm alone by myself," making a small hand gesture.
Ross turns and walks toward the door, pausing near a dartboard cabinet to finish, "then totally, totally understand."
Phoebe looks toward Joey, who stands with his arms crossed.
Phoebe steps forward, placing a hand on her chest, and says, "You know what? I can stay. I'm going to stay. Because the last time I went skiing I was too afraid to"
Phoebe waves her hand in a circle as she finishes, "jump off the chairlift. I just went round and round."
Joey uncrosses his arms and places a hand on Phoebe's arm.
Joey says, "Phoebe, we kind of need you to drive everybody up there in your grandmother's cab."
Joey turns to the others, extending his hand, and says, "But you know what? I'll stay." Monica steps in, placing her hands on Joey's chest, and says, "No, I'll stay."
Monica turns to Ross and says, "He's my brother." Ross turns to her and asks, "What, a pity stay?" Monica replies, "No,"
Monica smiles and steps closer to Ross.
Monica says, "we're going to have fun," gesturing with her hands.
Monica keeps her hands slightly raised.
Monica smiles and adds, "We can make fudge."
Ross stares at Monica with a still expression.
Ross asks, "Pity food."
Monica's smile fades slightly.
Ross looks down briefly, then says, "You know what?"
Ross raises a hand with his palm out, saying, "That's okay. All right? I don't need any of you to stay, okay? Nobody stays."
Chandler, seated at a table with a cigarette in hand, leans back and says, "Well, then I might as well offer to stay."
Monica and Ross turn their heads toward Chandler in silence. The scene changes to a dark, snow-lined road at night, where a yellow cab drives toward the right.
Inside the moving cab, Phoebe drives with Rachel in the passenger seat. Chandler, Monica, and Joey sit in the back.
Joey leans forward and asks, "Does anybody else feel bad about Ross?" Monica turns her head toward Joey and replies, "Why, do you think he's still mad at us?"
Chandler, holding an unlit cigarette, looks at Joey and says, "Well, he's probably more mad since you called him this morning to borrow his goggles."
Joey frowns slightly.
Joey replies, "Mine aren't tinted."
Chandler brings his cigarette to his lips. Phoebe glances up at the rearview mirror.
Looking at the mirror, Phoebe says, "Chandler?"
Chandler asks, "What?"
Phoebe asks, "What does the sign say?"
Chandler looks toward the clear partition separating the front and back seats.
Chandler traces a finger on the clear partition glass and reads, "Beam me up, Jesus."
Phoebe turns her head slightly toward the right.
Phoebe says, "The no smoking sign. There's no smoking in my grandmother's cab."
Chandler stares forward.
Chandler lowers his cigarette and says, "Okay, well, then I have to go to the bathroom."
Phoebe keeps her eyes on the road.
Phoebe replies, "No. No one's scheduled stops."
Phoebe continues, "You can go when we stop for gas." Chandler leans forward, pointing ahead, and says, "Okay, come on. There's a rest stop right up there. Come on. I really have to go."
Joey shifts in his seat, leaning his head back and groaning.
Joey winces and says, "Well, now I have to go."
Phoebe looks straight ahead.
Phoebe asks, "Is that all that it takes?"
Joey replies, "Sometimes. Just a small breeze."
The group remains quiet in the moving cab. Outside, the cab drives past a street sign along the snowy road.
The cab pulls into a snowy parking area next to a small lit building.
Inside the cab, Phoebe says, "Here we go. Can't brace yourself."
Chandler asks, "What?"
Phoebe hits the brakes. The passengers jolt forward sharply as a thud is heard.
Phoebe says, "Okay,"
Joey holds his nose and says, "Ow." Chandler opens the rear door, exits the cab, and walks toward the building.
Inside the cab, Monica says, "Go." Rachel shakes her head and replies, "No,"
Rachel says, "thank you." Monica steps out of the cab and says, "No, Rachel never pees in public restrooms."
Rachel, remaining in the front passenger seat, says, "Well, they never have any paper in there, you know? So my rule is"
Rachel finishes, "no tissue, no tushy."
Phoebe nods slightly and smiles while looking forward.
Rachel adjusts her hair and looks out the side window as they wait in the cab.
Inside a yellow taxi at night, Rachel opens the passenger door. "Well, if everybody's going," she says, stepping out of the vehicle.
Rachel walks away from the open taxi door. Outside in the snowy setting, Joey, Chandler, and Monica stand near a building.

Rachel quickly turns back toward the taxi, reaching for the open door. "Oh, you know what? Don't close it. The keys are in there," she says. Chandler hurries forward with an unlit cigarette in his mouth and says, "Oh, no." Phoebe, standing near Rachel, asks, "What's going on?" Chandler adds, "My lighter's in"
"...there," Chandler finishes, pointing at the locked taxi. The group gathers around the car. Chandler walks to the rear of the taxi.
Chandler crouches near the taxi's exhaust pipe, still holding the cigarette in his mouth. He stands back up and says, "Damn."
Chandler turns to the group. "tailpipe's not hot enough to light this," he says.
Joey steps forward toward the taxi.
Joey raises his hands and says, "Relax."
Gesturing toward the taxi door, Joey adds, "Okay? I can get this open."
Joey asks, "Anybody get a coat hanger?" Chandler replies, "Oh, I do."
Chandler pauses briefly, then adds, "Oh, no, wait a minute. I took it out of my shirt when I put it on this morning."
Joey closes his eyes briefly. Monica turns her head to look at Chandler.
Monica asks Chandler, "So if your parents hadn't got divorced you'd be able to answer a question like a normal person?"
Chandler stares at Monica in silence. Joey rubs his hands together.
Joey gestures with his hands and says, "Look, I just need a wire."
"Something to jimmy it," Joey continues. He then points toward the women and says, "Oh, hey, one of you guys give me the underwire from your bra."
Monica, Rachel, and Phoebe exclaim simultaneously, "What?"
The women look at Joey and cross their arms.
Joey gestures with his hands again. "Come on. Who has the biggest boobs? Please."
The women maintain their crossed arms and stare at Joey.
Joey holds his hands out, reasoning, "Whoever has the biggest boobs has the biggest bra."
"Therefore has the biggest wire," Joey concludes. Rachel keeps her arms crossed and says, "No, not getting my bra."
Joey says, "If you want to get back in the car, we need that wire. Your call."
The women stand silently with their arms crossed. Phoebe looks around the group.
Phoebe gestures toward Monica and says, "Okay, Monica's are the biggest."
Monica uncrosses her arms and her eyes widen.
Monica touches her chest defensively. "These tiny, little non breasts?" she says.
Pointing toward Rachel, Monica adds, "Please. Gotta be."
Rachel steps back slightly, reacting to Monica's gesture.
Rachel touches her chest and says, "No, no, mine are deceptively small."
Rachel continues, "I mean, I actually sometimes stuff my bra."
Phoebe looks intently at Rachel.
Phoebe responds, "All right, well, then your bra would still be big."
Rachel gestures vaguely with her hands.
Rachel motions in front of her chest and says, "No, I stuff outside the bra."
Chandler holds his hands out, gesturing between the women. "Compromise. Okay. Phoebe rachel, take off."
"Monica's bra," Chandler finishes.
Monica stares at Chandler silently.
Phoebe says, "All right, forget it. Never mind. Have mine," and begins maneuvering her arms inside her jacket.
Joey nods and says, "Thank you."
Chandler turns to the side and tosses a small item into the snow.
Monica points and asks, "Chandler, what are you doing? There is a trash can right there."
Chandler turns back to Monica and replies, "Well, I thought if I littered, that crying Indian might come by and save us."
Monica looks at Chandler. Phoebe pulls her arm out of her jacket sleeve, extracting her bra.
Holding up a large blue bra, Phoebe says, "Okay, here."
Joey takes the bra from Phoebe's hands.
Joey examines the bra and says, "Well, thank you, Phoebe. That is very generous."
Monica and Rachel step closer to look at the bra.
Rachel touches the fabric of the bra and says, "Wow, this is nice."
Monica turns her gaze to Phoebe.
Monica asks Phoebe, "Were you planning on meeting somebody up there?"
Chandler gestures with both hands and says, "Okay, now let's decide who has the nicest ass."
Joey inserts the underwire of the bra into the window seal of the taxi's front passenger door and pushes it downward.
Joey pulls up the door lock and says, "And there you go,"
The women cheer and quickly enter the taxi. Joey opens the driver-side door, and Chandler gets into the back seat.
Inside the taxi, Rachel looks back and says, "Chandler." Chandler, smoking a lit cigarette in the back seat, replies, "At least let me smoke it to the good part."
The scene changes to complete blackness. The audio becomes silent.
The screen remains completely black and silent until the end of the segment.
